title: Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)
tagline: Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna) — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Ranganathaswamy temple or Sri Ranganathaswamy temple in Srirangapatna, in the Mandya district of Karnataka state, India, is dedicated to the Hindu god Ranganatha (a manifestati
overview.description: The Ranganathaswamy temple or Sri Ranganathaswamy temple in Srirangapatna, in the Mandya district of Karnataka state, India, is dedicated to the Hindu god Ranganatha (a manifestation of Vishnu). The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition. It is one of the five important pilgrimage sites of Smarthas and Sri Vaishnavism along the river Kaveri for devotees of Ranganatha. These five sites are collectively known as Pancharanga Kshetrams in South India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Tipu Sultan Fort
topPlaces.description: Tipu Sultan's Fort is a historical fort located in Srirangapatna, the historical capital city of the Kingdom of Mysore in present-day South Indian state of Karnataka. Built by the Timmanna Nayaka in 1454, the fort was modified by King Haider Ali & King Tipu Sultan and fully fortified in the late 18th century with the help of French architects.

topPlaces.name: Obelisk Monument
topPlaces.description: Obelisk Monument, also known as the Siege Monument, is a commemorative edifice built in Srirangapatna in the Indian state of Karnataka. It marks the memory of the British officers and native soldiers who lost their lives during the siege of Srirangapatna on 4 May 1799 during the last Fourth Anglo-Mysore war fought during 1798-99 between the British army and the forces of the Mysore Kingdom led by Tipu Sultan.

topPlaces.name: Garrison Cemetery, Seringapatam
topPlaces.description: Garrison Cemetery is located in Seringapatam, on the banks of the river Cauvery, about 300m from the Bangalore Mysore Highway. It has about 307 graves of the European officers killed in the final assault on Tippu Sultan in 1799, and their family members.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Seringapatam (1799)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Seringapatam (5 April – 4 May 1799) was the final confrontation of the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War between the British East India Company and the Kingdom of Mysore. The British, with the allied Nizam Ali Khan, 2nd Nizam of Hyderabad and Maratha Empire, achieved a decisive victory after breaching the walls of the fortress at Seringapatam and storming the citadel.

topPlaces.name: Scott's Bungalow
topPlaces.description: The Scott's Bungalow is located in Seringapatam on the banks of the river Cauvery, at about half a mile from the Mysore Gate of the Seringapatam gate. The bungalow was the residence of Col.

topPlaces.name: Kirangur
topPlaces.description: Kirangur is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Srirangapatna taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Achappana Koppalu
topPlaces.description: Achappana Koppalu is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shrirangapattana taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Srirangapatna
topPlaces.description: Srirangapatna or Srirangapattana is a town and headquarters of one of the seven Taluks of Mandya district, in the Indian State of Karnataka. It gets its name from the Ranganathaswamy temple consecrated around 984 CE. Later, under the British rule, the city was renamed to Seringapatam.

items.note: Tipu Sultan's Fort is a historical fort located in Srirangapatna, the historical capital city of the Kingdom of Mysore in present-day South Indian state of Karnataka. Built by the Timmanna Nayaka in 1454, the fort was modified by King Haider Ali & King Tipu Sultan and fully fortified in the late 18th century with the help of French architects.

The British, with the allied Nizam Ali Khan, 2nd Nizam of Hyderabad and Maratha Empire, achieved a decisive victory after breaching the walls of the fortress at Seringapatam and storming the citadel.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna) is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (13 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna) at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna) start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangapatna)?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tipu Sultan Fort, Obelisk Monument, Garrison Cemetery, Seringapatam, Siege of Seringapatam (1799), Scott's Bungalow.
